expectations.text: Includes both feed ad and story ad copy
expectations.evidence: 3 feed ad variations and 3 story ad variations provided, with clear format distinction.

expectations.text: Feed ad opens with a strong first-line hook
expectations.evidence: Strong hooks: 'You've seen the claims. "Glass skin in 3 days." "Erases 10 years overnight."' / 'Most serums shout. This one works.' / 'It won't make you look 22 again.' All scroll-stopping for the skeptic audience.

expectations.text: Copy focuses on skin benefits, not just ingredient specs
expectations.evidence: Balances both: lists ingredients for credibility but frames them as benefits ('neutralizes free radical damage, evens out dark spots over weeks, strengthens your skin barrier'). Benefits lead, ingredients support.

expectations.text: No region-specific slang or idioms (global audience safe)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly noted as using Global English. No idioms, no culture-specific references. Notes section even suggests localized variants for East Asian markets.

expectations.text: Includes a clear call to action
expectations.evidence: Clear CTAs with price: 'Shop now at glowtheory.com', '$38 → glowtheory.com', 'Swipe up to shop'. Price included for transparency.
6 total variations: 3 feed (Skeptic's Choice, The Quiet Serum, What It Won't Do) + 3 story (Swipe-Through Honesty, The Anti-Ad, Ingredient-First). Each with named strategic approach.
